messages.content:
Aphagia, or anorexia, develops in experimental animals in case of:

Select one or more:

Stimulation of the lateral hypothalamus

Destruction of the lateral hypothalamus

Stimulation of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Destruction of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Destruction of the lateral hypothalamus
Stimulation of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Choose all the effects of the sympathetic ANS part stimulation among listed:

Select one or more:

Inhibition of gastric juice secretion

Inhibition of pancreatic juice secretion

Stimulation of the gastric motility (motor activity)

Stimulation of secretion of fluid saliva in big amount

Relaxation of gastrointestinal sphincters

Stimulation of secretion of thick viscous saliva in small amount

Inhibition of gastric juice secretion
Inhibition of pancreatic juice secretion
Stimulation of the gastric motility (motor activity)
Relaxation of gastrointestinal sphincters

Choose among the digestive system functions only its NON-DIGESTIVE functions:

Select one or more:

Secretory function

Protective function

Vitamins formation

Motor function

Antitoxic function

Metabolic function

Excretory function

Protective function
Antitoxic function

Choose the functions of hydrochloric acid of the gastric juice:

Select one or more:

Stimulation of intestinal secretion and motility

Bactericidal action

Breakdown of proteins into peptides

Activation of pepsinogen

Proteins denaturation

Bactericidal action
Breakdown of proteins into peptides
Activation of pepsinogen
Proteins denaturation

Choose the functions of saliva:

Select one or more:

Digestion of proteins

Lubrication of food pieces

Initial digestion of starch

Supply of Ca2+ ions for teeth enamel

Protection due to bactericidal properties

Lubrication of food pieces
Initial digestion of starch
Supply of Ca2+ ions for teeth enamel
Protection due to bactericidal properties

Choose the functions of the stomach:

Select one or more:

Deposition of food

Digestion of fats

Initial digestion of proteins

Complete digestion of proteins

Mixing of a chyme

Periodic emptying

Initial digestion of proteins
Mixing of chyme
Periodic emptying

Choose the substance that provides LONG-TERM regulation of appetite:

Select one:

Cholecystokinin

Ghrelin

Leptin

Insulin

Peptide YY
Leptin

Choose the substance that stimulates appetite:

Select one:

Ghrelin

Insulin

Peptide YY

Cholecystokinin

Leptin
Ghrelin

Choose the substances which suppress appetite and inhibit food intake:

Select one or more:

Ghrelin

Insulin

Cholecystokinin

Leptin

Peptide YY

Cholecystokinin
Leptin
Peptide YY

Gastrin is produced by:

Select one:

G cells of the stomach pyloric part

G cells of the stomach body

Parietal (oxyntic) cells of the stomach body

Mucous cells

Chief cells of the stomach body
G cells of the stomach pyloric part

Hyperphagia, or bulimia, develops in experimental animals in case of:

Select one or more:

Destruction of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Destruction of the lateral hypothalamus

Stimulation of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Stimulation of the lateral hypothalamus
Destruction of the ventromedial hypothalamus

Pepsin is produced by:

Select one:

Chief cells of the stomach body

Mucous cells

Parietal (oxyntic) cells

Chief cells of the stomach pyloric part
Chief cells of the stomach body

The center of hunger is located in:

Select one:

Lateral hypothalamus

Posterior hypothalamus

Thalamus

Medulla

Ventromedial hypothalamus
Lateral hypothalamus

The center of satiety is located in:

Select one:

Ventromedial hypothalamus

Medulla

Thalamus

Posterior hypothalamus

Lateral hypothalamus
Ventromedial hypothalamus

The cephalic phase of gastric secretion occurs:

Select one:

When food is located in the stomach

Before food enters the stomach

After food is transported from the stomach to intestine
Before food enters the stomach
